Title: Ectomycorrhizal fungi recruit hyphae-associated bacteria that metabolize thiamine to promote pine symbiosis

Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified from fungal genome as a putative thiamine transporter; fungus lacks key thiamine biosynthesis genes and depends on exogenous thiamine
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Suillus clintonianus lacks key thiamine biosynthesis genes but retains putative thiamine transporter THI73. 2. THI73 expression increased 1.86-fold in co-culture with Bacillus altitudinis B4. 3. The transporter likely mediates uptake of bacterially secreted thiamine that supports fungal growth and mycorrhization.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in Suillus clintonianus during co-culture with Bacillus altitudinis B4
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes uptake of exogenous bacterial thiamine into fungal hyphae, alleviating thiamine auxotrophy
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Co-culture with Bacillus altitudinis B4; thiamine-deficient medium
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing of Suillus clintonianus: PRJNA1355154; RT-qPCR for expression
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome annotation, RT-qPCR, 13C12-thiamine uptake detection in fungal hyphae by FTICR-MS
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential vitamin cofactor supporting fungal growth and ectomycorrhizal establishment

Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supports bacterial recruitment to the fungal hyphosphere rather than direct thiamine catalysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. filG expression was upregulated 2.19-fold in Bacillus altitudinis B4 exposed to Suillus clintonianus exudates. 2. Its induction accompanied enhanced swimming and swarming motility. 3. Improved bacterial recruitment likely facilitates thiamine delivery to the fungus.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in Bacillus altitudinis B4 in response to Suillus clintonianus mycelial exudates
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances bacterial motility and chemotactic recruitment, indirectly promoting fungal thiamine acquisition
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exposure to fungal mycelial exudates; thiamine-deficient context
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in response to fungal exudates
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports fungal growth through bacterium-mediated vitamin provisioning

Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts in chemotactic sensing of fungal exudates, indirectly enabling establishment of thiamine-sharing interaction
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MCP expression was upregulated 1.66-fold in Bacillus altitudinis B4 exposed to Suillus clintonianus exudates. 2. This increase accompanied stronger chemotaxis and motility. 3. Enhanced sensing of fungal signals likely improves bacterial colonization and thiamine support to the fungus.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in Bacillus altitudinis B4 in response to Suillus clintonianus exudates
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes chemotactic sensing and recruitment of Bacillus altitudinis B4 toward fungal hyphae, indirectly supporting thiamine transfer
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exposure to fungal exudates; thiamine-deficient conditions
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in response to fungal exudates
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports fungal nutrition by facilitating bacterial delivery of thiamine

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in Bacillus altitudinis B4 under co-culture with Suillus clintonianus and ureidosuccinic acid treatment; thiamine quantification in bacterial supernatant by HPLC/fluorescence
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key thiamine biosynthesis gene; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Thiamine de novo biosynthesis, pyrimidine moiety branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Bacterial culture supernatant; absorbed into fungal hyphae
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression increased in combined fungus-bacterium system and after ureidosuccinic acid treatment, supporting stimulated bacterial thiamine production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in Bacillus altitudinis B4 during co-culture with Suillus clintonianus and under 0.5 mM ureidosuccinic acid
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR; no accession for bacterial gene expression dataset reported
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher thiD expression is associated with increased bacterial thiamine secretion that promotes Suillus clintonianus growth
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential vitamin cofactor supporting fungal growth and ectomycorrhizal establishment
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for biosynthesis of target product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Co-culture with Suillus clintonianus; ureidosuccinic acid supplementation; thiamine-deficient medium
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated under co-culture and ureidosuccinic acid treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. thiD expression was upregulated 4.01-fold in Bacillus altitudinis B4 during co-culture with Suillus clintonianus. 2. Ureidosuccinic acid significantly elevated thiD expression. 3. Increased thiD expression correlated with enhanced thiamine secretion that rescued fungal thiamine auxotrophy.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in Bacillus altitudinis B4 under co-culture with Suillus clintonianus; thiamine quantification in supernatant
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key thiamine biosynthesis gene; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Thiamine de novo biosynthesis, thiazole moiety branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Bacterial culture supernatant; absorbed into fungal hyphae
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Most strongly induced among tested thiamine genes in co-culture (6.44-fold)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in Bacillus altitudinis B4 during co-culture with Suillus clintonianus
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher thiG expression is associated with increased thiamine production by Bacillus altitudinis B4
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential vitamin cofactor supporting fungal growth
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for biosynthesis of target product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Co-culture with Suillus clintonianus in thiamine-deficient medium
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. thiG expression increased 6.44-fold in the combined treatment. 2. Its induction supports enhanced bacterial thiamine biosynthesis under fungal influence. 3. This contributed to rescue of fungal thiamine auxotrophy.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in Bacillus altitudinis B4 under co-culture with Suillus clintonianus; metabolite detection of thiamine
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key thiamine biosynthesis gene; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Thiamine de novo biosynthesis, pyrimidine moiety branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Bacterial culture supernatant; absorbed into fungal hyphae
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as key bacterial thiamine biosynthesis gene upregulated in the combined system
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in Bacillus altitudinis B4 during co-culture with Suillus clintonianus
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher thiC expression is associated with enhanced bacterial thiamine biosynthesis and improved fungal growth
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential vitamin cofactor supporting fungal growth and symbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Co-culture with Suillus clintonianus; thiamine deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. thiC expression was upregulated 5.94-fold in the combined treatment. 2. The result supports fungal stimulation of bacterial thiamine biosynthetic capacity. 3. Increased thiamine availability benefited fungal growth.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Thiaminase II/TenA family; thiamine salvage enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in Bacillus altitudinis B4 under co-culture with Suillus clintonianus and ureidosuccinic acid treatment; thiamine measurement
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key thiamine salvage gene; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Bacterial culture supernatant; absorbed into fungal hyphae
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Induced both in co-culture and by ureidosuccinic acid, suggesting activation of thiamine salvage in support of secretion
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in Bacillus altitudinis B4 during co-culture with Suillus clintonianus and under ureidosuccinic acid treatment
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates in salvage of degraded thiamine compounds to support thiamine production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher tenA expression is associated with increased thiamine availability to Suillus clintonianus
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential vitamin cofactor supporting fungal growth and ectomycorrhizal formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for salvage/recycling toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Co-culture with Suillus clintonianus; ureidosuccinic acid; thiamine-deficient medium
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated under co-culture and ureidosuccinic acid treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. tenA expression increased 5.25-fold in the combined treatment. 2. Ureidosuccinic acid further elevated tenA expression. 3. Activation of thiamine salvage likely contributed to bacterial support of fungal thiamine nutrition.
